Meet the Owner

Your neighbor in payments —
Not an 800 number.

Lisa has run her own business, so she knows what every percentage point on a statement actually costs you. That's the lens Aqua Leo runs through: fewer fees, faster funding, and a real person who picks up the phone — because our business is local, and so are we, located in downtown North Canton.

Whether you're ringing up regulars at a counter, rolling up to a festival in a food truck, sending monthly invoices, or quoting jobs from your truck, we'll set you up with the right equipment and answer your call long after the install.

— Lisa, Aqua Leo Payment Processing Solutions

Common Questions

What business owners ask before switching.

Restaurants and retail come to mind first, but we also work with e-commerce shops, home service companies, nonprofits, B2B businesses, and multi-location operations. If you accept payments, we can build a setup around it.

It depends on your current pricing, volume, and equipment — every business is different. Many clients save thousands a year through updated pricing, newer hardware, or a pass-the-fee program. A free cost review will show your real number before you commit to anything.

Most businesses are fully set up within 24–48 hours. We handle onboarding, hardware setup, data migration, and training, so there's no downtime in between.

No. We'd rather earn your business with good service and clear pricing than lock you in with paperwork. Most clients stick around because it works, not because they have to.

Three Ways to Price Processing

Most processors offer one model. We offer all three.

Different businesses, different math. Here's how each one works so you can pick what actually fits.

Model 01
Flat Rate
One predictable percentage on every sale. You absorb the fees; customers see one price.
Best fit: low-volume or online-first shops that want simplicity over savings.
  • One blended rate, easy to explain
  • No surprises on your statement
  • Higher effective cost than the others
Model 02
Interchange Plus
Wholesale card-network cost plus a small fixed markup. Maximum transparency, line by line.
Best fit: higher-volume merchants tired of vague "qualified vs. non-qualified" tiers.
  • True wholesale rate visible
  • Lower total cost as volume grows
  • Every component shown clearly
Model 03
Pass-the-Fee
The card-paying customer covers the processing cost. Comes in three flavors.
Best fit: brick-and-mortar where savings show up in the bank, not just on paper.
  • Dual Pricing — two prices, customer picks
  • Cash Discount / Admin Fee — small fee on cards
  • Surcharging — credit-only, state-regulated
The plain-English version: Dual Pricing is the cleanest path for most NE Ohio merchants — both prices are visible and there's no debit-card complication. Surcharging has the strictest rules but works well on high-ticket B2B. Cash Discount sits in the middle. We'll tell you which one actually fits your customers before you sign anything.
